0.label: Master Bath + Background Rooms
0.purpose: Slow Sunday pace: knock out the master bath on your own time and vacuum the two background rooms. Still plenty of room to rest.
tasks: Master bath: toilet, sink, mirror, shower, then vacuum and mop the floor.
Vacuum the office.
Vacuum the spare room.

1.label: Reset Day
1.purpose: Start the week by getting the house back to neutral so nothing feels chaotic.
tasks: Walk at the park before going home from work.
Pick up clutter.
Take out trash.
Do dishes or reset the sink and counters.
Quick straighten-up pass through the house.

2.label: Dusting Day
2.purpose: Knock out the dust in one full pass so the house feels cleaner fast.
tasks: Walk at the park before going home from work.
Dust the whole house in one go.
Hit furniture, picture frames, shelves, surfaces, and obvious dusty spots.

3.label: Baseboards Day
3.purpose: Handle the detail grime in one pass and keep the house from feeling grimy around the edges.
tasks: Walk at the park before going home from work.
Do all baseboards in one go.
Optional: catch trim, corners, or window sills if you still have gas in the tank.

4.label: Main Floors
4.purpose: Tackle the main living-zone floors plus the hallway in one pass before they get nasty.
tasks: Walk at the park before going home from work.
Vacuum living room, kitchen, and hallway.
Mop living room, kitchen, and hallway.

5.label: Main Bath
5.purpose: End the week with just the main bathroom. One small room, one focused pass, then you're done.
tasks: Walk at the park before going home from work.
Main bath: toilet, sink, mirror, tub, then vacuum and mop the floor.

6.label: Laundry + Bedroom Floors + House Project
6.purpose: Saturday is the main progress day: finish laundry completely, knock out the remaining floors, and do one house project.
tasks: Laundry all in one go: wash, dry, fold, and put away immediately.
Vacuum bedroom and laundry room.
Mop bedroom and laundry room.
Do one house project from the current rotation.

recurringExtras.task: Listen to a chapter of your Audible book.
Watch an educational / self-growth video (instead of TV or scrolling).

rotatingSaturdayProjects.title: Seal cracks or windows
rotatingSaturdayProjects.details: Silicone one window, crack area, or other sealing spot around the house.

rotatingSaturdayProjects.title: Landscaping cleanup
rotatingSaturdayProjects.details: Pull weeds, clean a bed, trim an area, or tidy up an outside section.

rotatingSaturdayProjects.title: Plant or improve a bed
rotatingSaturdayProjects.details: Plant something, edge an area, or make one visible improvement outside.

rotatingSaturdayProjects.title: Maintenance checkpoint
rotatingSaturdayProjects.details: Walk the house, inspect problem spots, and finish one repair or maintenance item.
